---
title: "Platelet WCM — Graphviz layout comparison"
author: "Steve Haigh"
date: "2026-05-14"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    geometry: "margin=2cm"
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "STIX Two Text"
    monofont: "Menlo"
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    urlcolor: blue
    toc: false
    include-in-header: ../pandoc-header.tex
---

# Graphviz vs Mermaid — same content, different layout engine

Same biology as `../archive/model-status-2026-05-13.qmd`, rendered through Graphviz
`dot` instead of Mermaid. The advantage Graphviz brings:

1. **Visible compartment boxes** via `subgraph cluster_*` — closer to a
   cell-biology drawing than Mermaid can manage.
2. **`rank=same` control** — receptors actually sit on one row, buffers
   on another, regardless of edge density.
3. **Nested clusters** — the DTS and mitochondrial sub-compartments sit
   *inside* the cytosol cluster, matching the cell topology.

```{dot}
//| fig-width: 7.5
//| fig-cap: "Platelet WCM v0.4.1 — Ca²⁺ pathway (Graphviz layout). Compartments rendered as labelled boxes; same-row clusters forced via rank=same."

digraph platelet_wcm {
    graph [
        rankdir = TB
        splines = true
        nodesep = 0.3
        ranksep = 0.45
        compound = true
        fontname = "Helvetica"
        fontsize = 11
        bgcolor = white
    ]
    node [
        shape = box
        style = "rounded,filled"
        fontname = "Helvetica"
        fontsize = 9
        margin = "0.12,0.06"
    ]
    edge [
        fontname = "Helvetica"
        fontsize = 8
        arrowsize = 0.6
        color = "#444444"
    ]

    // ════════ Compartment: extracellular (top) ════════
    subgraph cluster_ext_top {
        label = "Extracellular space"
        style = "rounded,filled"
        bgcolor = "#fafafa"
        color = "#888888"
        fontsize = 11

        STIM   [label="AGONIST INPUT\nThrombin 1 nM · ADP 10 µM", shape=oval, style="dashed,filled", fillcolor=white]
        CAEX   [label="Ca²⁺_ex\n1.2 mM", shape=ellipse, fillcolor="#fce4a6", penwidth=2]
        ATPEX  [label="ATP_ex\ngranule release", fillcolor="#fce4a6"]

        {rank=same; STIM; CAEX; ATPEX}
    }

    // ════════ Compartment: PM (receptors + entry channels) ════════
    subgraph cluster_pm_top {
        label = "Plasma membrane — receptors + entry"
        style = "rounded,filled"
        bgcolor = "#fef0f8"
        color = "#888888"
        fontsize = 11

        P2Y1   [label="P2Y1\n(ADP) · 150",                    fillcolor="#fcb6e0"]
        PAR1   [label="PAR1\n(thrombin hi) · 2 500",          fillcolor="#fcb6e0"]
        PAR4   [label="PAR4\n(thrombin lo) · 500",            fillcolor="#fcb6e0"]
        ORAI1  [label="Orai1\nSOCE · 1 447",                  fillcolor="#a6d8fc"]
        P2X1   [label="P2X1\nATP-gated · 1 000",              fillcolor="#a6d8fc"]

        {rank=same; P2Y1; PAR1; PAR4; ORAI1; P2X1}
    }

    // ════════ Compartment: cytosol (with DTS and mito nested) ════════
    subgraph cluster_cyt {
        label = "Cytosol — 6 fL · ~100 nM free Ca²⁺"
        style = "rounded,filled"
        bgcolor = "#fcfff0"
        color = "#888888"
        fontsize = 11

        GQ     [label="Gαq-GTP\n~100 of 5 000",     fillcolor="#fcd4a6"]
        PLCB   [label="PLCβ active\n857 total",     fillcolor="#fcd4a6"]
        PIP2   [label="PIP₂ · 112 000",             fillcolor="#e6e6e6"]
        IP3    [label="IP₃ · 50 nM rest",           fillcolor="#fff8a6"]
        DAG    [label="DAG",                        fillcolor="#fff8a6"]

        CACYT  [label="Ca²⁺_cyt\n~100 nM free\n~85% buffered", shape=ellipse, fillcolor="#ffd56b", penwidth=2.5]

        CAM    [label="CaM ladder\n20k free",       fillcolor="#a6fcc4"]
        GSN    [label="Gelsolin proxy\n1.4M sites", fillcolor="#a6fcc4"]

        // ─── DTS (nested) ───
        subgraph cluster_dts {
            label = "DTS lumen — 0.26 fL · ~250 µM free"
            style = "rounded,filled"
            bgcolor = "#eef9f3"
            color = "#666666"
            fontsize = 10

            IP3R   [label="IP3R · 1 328\nDYK + Nernst\nγ = 0.075 pS", fillcolor="#a6d8fc"]
            SERCA  [label="SERCA\n6-state E1/E2",                     fillcolor="#d4a6fc"]
            STIM1  [label="STIM1 sensor\n438 free",                   fillcolor="#fca6a6"]

            CADTS  [label="Ca²⁺_DTS\n~250 µM free\n~95% buffered",
                    shape=ellipse, fillcolor="#ffd56b", penwidth=2.5]

            CALR   [label="CALR · 528k",     fillcolor="#a6fcc4"]
            HSP    [label="HSP90B1 · 150k",  fillcolor="#a6fcc4"]
            BIP    [label="BiP · 50k",       fillcolor="#a6fcc4"]
            CREC   [label="CREC · 60k",      fillcolor="#a6fcc4"]

            {rank=same; IP3R; SERCA; STIM1}
            {rank=same; CALR; HSP; BIP; CREC}
        }

        // ─── Mito (nested) ───
        subgraph cluster_mito {
            label = "Mitochondria"
            style = "rounded,filled"
            bgcolor = "#f8efff"
            color = "#666666"
            fontsize = 10

            MCU     [label="MCU uptake\nHill, K=1µM",  fillcolor="#d4a6fc"]
            CAMITO  [label="Ca²⁺_mito",                fillcolor="#fce4a6"]
            NCLX    [label="NCLX release",             fillcolor="#d4a6fc"]

            {rank=same; MCU; CAMITO; NCLX}
            MCU -> CAMITO -> NCLX [style=invis]
        }
    }

    // ════════ Compartment: PM (extrusion) ════════
    subgraph cluster_pm_bot {
        label = "Plasma membrane — extrusion"
        style = "rounded,filled"
        bgcolor = "#fef0f8"
        color = "#888888"
        fontsize = 11

        PMCA   [label="PMCA\nbasal + CaM-act · 1 400", fillcolor="#d4a6fc"]
        NCX    [label="NCX\nNa⁺/Ca²⁺ exch.",           fillcolor="#d4a6fc"]

        {rank=same; PMCA; NCX}
    }

    // ════════ Edges ════════
    // Signal cascade (dashed = regulatory / activation)
    STIM  -> P2Y1  [style=dashed, label="ADP"]
    STIM  -> PAR1  [style=dashed, label="thrombin"]
    STIM  -> PAR4  [style=dashed, label="thrombin"]
    P2Y1  -> GQ    [style=dashed, label="GDP→GTP"]
    PAR1  -> GQ    [style=dashed]
    PAR4  -> GQ    [style=dashed]
    GQ    -> PLCB  [style=dashed, label="activates"]
    PLCB  -> PIP2  [style=dashed, label="hydrolyses"]
    PIP2  -> IP3
    PIP2  -> DAG
    IP3   -> IP3R  [style=dashed, label="activates"]
    IP3R  -> CACYT [label="Ca²⁺ release"]

    // Cyt buffering
    CACYT -> CAM   [dir=both, style=dashed, label="buffer"]
    CACYT -> GSN   [dir=both, style=dashed, label="buffer"]

    // SERCA → DTS
    CACYT -> SERCA [label="SERCA"]
    SERCA -> CADTS

    // DTS buffering
    CADTS -> CALR  [dir=both, style=dashed]
    CADTS -> HSP   [dir=both, style=dashed]
    CADTS -> BIP   [dir=both, style=dashed]
    CADTS -> CREC  [dir=both, style=dashed]

    // SOCE
    CADTS -> STIM1 [style=dashed, label="senses"]
    STIM1 -> ORAI1 [style=dashed, label="gates"]

    // Mito shuttle
    CACYT -> MCU
    NCLX  -> CACYT

    // PM extrusion (down)
    CACYT -> PMCA
    CACYT -> NCX
    CAM   -> PMCA  [style=dashed, label="Ca₄·CaM"]
    PMCA  -> CAEX  [constraint=false]
    NCX   -> CAEX  [constraint=false]

    // PM entry (back up)
    CAEX  -> ORAI1
    CAEX  -> P2X1
    ATPEX -> P2X1  [style=dashed, label="gates"]
    ORAI1 -> CACYT [label="SOCE refill"]
    P2X1  -> CACYT [label="fast entry"]
}
```

---

## Notes for comparison

**Wins over the Mermaid version:**

- Compartment boxes are labelled and visible — the BioRender redraw
  now has explicit "draw this box around these proteins" instructions.
- The DTS sub-cluster sits inside the cytosol cluster, which is the
  correct topology (DTS lumen is enclosed by DTS membrane which lives
  in the cytosol).
- The 7+ edges leaving Ca²⁺_cyt no longer trigger Mermaid's "spread
  everything horizontally" failure mode — Graphviz respects the cluster
  hierarchy.

**Costs vs Mermaid:**

- GitHub does **not** render Graphviz natively in `.md` files. Mermaid
  does. So for docs that need to be browsable on GitHub, Mermaid wins.
  For PDF-only docs (this one), Graphviz is the better tool.
- DOT syntax is less readable than Mermaid for casual review. If the
  diagram needs to be hand-edited by someone unfamiliar with Graphviz,
  Mermaid is friendlier.
- No sequence/gantt/state diagrams in Graphviz — it does graphs only.
